text: Leisure: growth in an uncertain world. [Sector forecast](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/sectorprognoses/index.html). [Leisure](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/leisure/index.html). 23/06/2025 • 5 minutes reading. [Gerarda Westerhuis](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/authors/gerarda-westerhuis/index.html). The leisure sector will grow by 1.5 percent in volume in 2025 and by another 1 percent in 2026. The enormous peaks and valleys surrounding the corona years are over and the figures are normalizing.

metadata.source_title: Leisure: growth in an uncertain world

text: Travel organizations and Amsterdam hotels are relatively dependent on American guests.

metadata.source_title: Leisure: growth in an uncertain world

text: Further diversifying target groups helps to spread risks and increase the resilience of the sector.

metadata.source_title: Leisure: growth in an uncertain world

text: View the forecasts of the leisure sector in 2 minutes. In this video, sector economist Gerarda Westerhuis talks in 2 minutes about the growth expectations in leisure for 2025 and 2026. See what challenges the leisure sector faces and what you can do as an entrepreneur. [Go to the video](/en/business/insights/sectors-and-trends/sectorprognoses/leisure/growth-leisure-sector-by-increasing-purchasing-power-and-tourism.html). The growth of the sector is driven by an increase in purchasing power and growth in tourism. Consumers may become more cautious in their spending due to geopolitical uncertainties.

metadata.source_title: Leisure: growth in an uncertain world

text: We do not yet see this reflected in the turnover figures, which showed growth for the catering industry in the first quarter of 2025, partly thanks to the warm spring month of March. For 2026, the VAT increase on overnight stays from 9 to 21 percent will be higher than the sector. As a result, the competitive position of Dutch accommodations may deteriorate compared to those in Belgium and Germany, where the VAT rate is 6 and 7 percent respectively. The increase puts pressure on the margins at accommodations that cannot pass on these costs.

metadata.source_title: Leisure: growth in an uncertain world

text:
Necessary reinvestments are then brought forward. We previously described pressure on margins and other challenges in the leisure subsector forecasts. [Read also: Growth in leisure is normalizing; challenges remain](/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/sectorprognoses/leisure/groei-leisure-normaliserings-dagen-spelen.html)
Travel industry relatively high dependence on the US. While US tariffs on goods will not have a direct impact on the sector, the broader diplomatic and economic fallout could also extend to services such as tourism and business travel. It is estimated that the added value that Dutch travel organizations create thanks to exports to the US amounts to more than 1 billion euros. This concerns American tourists and business travelers who go on holiday in the Netherlands or Europe and book their trip through a Dutch travel organization. This arranges hotel, transport, guides and excursions for American visitors. This added value of more than 1 billion euros accounts for 12.8 percent of the total exports of the travel industry. Dependence on Amsterdam hotels and recreation. The dependence on American travelers is particularly high in Amsterdam. The share of Americans in the number of guests that Amsterdam hotels receive is high at 11 percent. Americans spend an above-average number of nights in the capital; of the more than 1.5 million American guests in the Netherlands, 65 percent stay in a hotel in Amsterdam; for Germans and Belgians this percentage is 27 and 21 percent respectively. Americans relatively often go to 4 and 5 star hotels; 70 percent of guests in Amsterdam stay in these highest classes. For all guests this is more than 55 percent, data from the Central Bureau of Statistics (CBS) show. American guests like to visit museums, shops and the catering industry. For example, 93 percent of Americans who visited the Netherlands in 2024 indicated that they would undertake sightseeing as an activity. This is evident from the Survey of International Air Travelers, which has been collecting statistical data on air passenger travelers to and from the United States (US) since 1985.

metadata.source_title: Leisure: growth in an uncertain world

text: While sightseeing is a very broad concept, the other activities mentioned are more concrete. For example, 81 percent of respondents indicated that they would go shopping and 70 percent would visit a museum or art gallery.

metadata.source_title: Leisure: growth in an uncertain world

text: Fine dining also scored high at 44 percent.

metadata.source_title: Leisure: growth in an uncertain world

text: Although Americans still plan to travel in the next 12 months, 80 percent say they will change their travel behavior due to recent developments, according to a consumer survey from MMGY Travel Intelligence in April this year. They indicate that they opt for domestic rather than international destinations, cheaper means of transport and shorter stays. The weaker dollar also makes holidays in Europe more expensive for American tourists. The Netherlands will also become less attractive as a destination. Fewer dollars per euro means less purchasing power and therefore lower spending in catering, museums and shops.

metadata.source_title: Leisure: growth in an uncertain world

text: Traveling to the USA. A decline in demand for travel from the Netherlands to the US due to political unrest, dissatisfaction with Donald Trump's policies and stricter border controls is already visible. Short city trips to New York, for example, remain popular, but interest in longer tours is decreasing.

metadata.source_title: Leisure: growth in an uncertain world

text: Apparently the objections mentioned do not outweigh the lower dollar exchange rate, which actually makes staying in the US cheaper. American and international airlines and hotel groups are feeling the impact of the reduced demand, but Dutch travel organizations that generate a large part of their turnover from organizing trips to the US may also notice it.

metadata.source_title: Leisure: growth in an uncertain world
Leisure: growth in an uncertain world

text: Trump's return has led to renewed discussions about nationalism, immigration and foreign policy. While the number of American travelers to Europe may be declining, this unrest may mean that other target groups, such as Canadians and LGBTQ people, who object to traveling to the US, may show more interest in a holiday in Europe. In addition, demand for travel within Europe remains strong. For example, the Dutch Tourism and Conventions Board (NBTC) expects the number of Spaniards to double between 2023 and 2035, from more than 800,000 to more than 1.6 million, partly due to the relatively good economic prospects in the country. Spaniards are therefore an interesting target group. Further diversifying target groups can help spread the risks and increase the resilience of the sector.

text: The recovery has started with the phasing out of the measures regarding corona.

metadata.source_title: Leisure: growth in an uncertain world

text:
The terraces are starting to fill up, the number of tourists is increasing and events can be organized again. Staff shortages are now the biggest challenge. Digitizing staff work and spreading guests can limit the impact. [View all articles](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/leisure/index.html)
